input.testCaseTitle: Process refund with split tender — gift card + credit card remainder
input.stepLabel: Submit refund — expect 200 + matched amounts on both tenders
input.errorMessage: AssertionError: Expected refund amount on gift card tender to equal 240.00, got 239.99. Total refund 480.00 did NOT match sum(tenderAmounts) = 479.99.
input.stackTrace:
AssertionError: Expected refund amount on gift card tender to equal 240.00, got 239.99
  at Object.<anonymous> (apps/e2e/specs/refund-split-tender.spec.ts:142:23)
  at processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:96:5)
  at refund.service.ts:142 splitTenderRefund.allocate()
  at refund.service.ts:108 buildTenderAmounts()
kbContext: Refund service uses fixed-point arithmetic only at the API boundary; internal math passes through Number multiplication which loses cent precision at >2 decimal allocations.
Iksula uses 50:50 split-tender weighting for gift-card + credit-card refunds where original purchase split was symmetric.

groundTruth.rootCauseDetail: Floating-point precision loss in refund.service.ts:142 splitTenderRefund.allocate() — gift card tender allocation uses (total * 0.5) Number math which produces 239.995 → 239.99 after toFixed(2) truncation. Should use integer minor-units (paise) arithmetic throughout.
groundTruth.notesForEval: Top-2 hit if Sherlock returns code-bug OR data-bug in either slot 1 or slot 2. Sherlock should ideally cite refund.service.ts:142. data-bug acceptable because a senior reviewer could reasonably say the bug is in how the data (amount precision) is represented vs how the code multiplies it.
